mentions.note: Think self-driving vehicles and robots (big growth engines), not just cars; Elon Musk may merge with SpaceX (dual-class structure allows escape from Tesla shareholder noise); if doesn't merge, Meta and Berkshire may lose trillion-dollar status.
Magnificent Seven member (but Cramer's charitable trust held only 6 of 7, not Tesla initially); borrowed heavily for growth with massive opportunity.
Higher-risk growth stock; appropriate for discretionary portfolios with capital to risk, not suitable as core retirement holding.
Robots and full self-driving cars are revolutionary; selling future. Cramer buyer not seller. Short-sellers missing the move.
Market wants self-driving, robots, SpaceX IPO insights—not auto sales; reports at close; focus on next-gen innovation narratives.
Q1 deliveries miss guidance; deteriorating auto business, weak energy storage. SpaceX IPO removes scarcity value. Expect drift lower unless robotaxi/robot progress or core business turns around.
Beat numbers but EV focus irrelevant; future about cybercabs and robots—higher capex than expected; down 3%; Musk acolytes prefer SpaceX IPO.
Cyber cabin robot company, not pure car play; XAI merger with SpaceX immaterial; hasn't blasted off; General Motors more magnificent than Tesla.
Repositioned from car company to robotics and cyber company juggernaut. Paper became scissors; now a buyer. AI+humanoid robot upside outweighs automotive headwinds.
Top and bottom line beat on Q4; 1.4B free cash flow impressive; robotaxi and Optimus rollout look ahead of expectations; strong after-hours move despite known production shortfalls.
Reports Wednesday but trades on narrative not numbers. EV business weak. Musk credibility on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (2027 launch) paramount — needs concrete progress updates.
Repeat: robot company, autonomous play, not car company. Quarter could define narrative; stock soars if they reframe as non-car company.
Part of Magnificent 7; experiencing P/E compression like peers but company too strong to bet against long-term; stay the course.
Up 11% after volatile 2025; core EV business 'awful,' earnings down 60% from 2022, but investors focus on robotaxis/humanoid robots; Musk must deliver on both fronts to sustain bull case.
